title: senna glutinosa subsp. pruinosa
intro: Senna glutinosa subsp. pruinosa, also known as the Asian senna or pruinose senna, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, native to Southeast Asia and India. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its laxative properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of anthraquinone glycosides. The medicinal properties of Senna glutinosa subsp. pruinosa have been found to promote bowel movements, relieve constipation, and support digestive health, making it a popular natural remedy for gastrointestinal issues. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for consumption, and its dried leaves and pods are also used in herbal teas and supplements.
